---
title: "LRPLF08: Level factors for TROG receptive grammar (T)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by a LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Opus 4.8).
:::

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}

This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model evolves and further data are received.

If the model was not fitted in "reporting" mode, the results will be more approximate than where additional sampling has been performed.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

import pandas as pd

_here = Path(".")
with open(_here / "config.json") as f:
    config = json.load(f)
factor_summary = pd.read_csv(_here / "factor_summary.csv")
```

## Overview

LRPLF08 is a **level-factors** model for TROG receptive grammar (`trog`): what is associated with the score *level* at each of the four timepoints (Beta-Binomial logit, child random intercept; no own baseline).

::: {.callout-important title="Causal labelling + level-model crossover caveat"}

Every non-randomised coefficient is an **adjusted association** under the DAG. The group effect is `group x time` (a per-timepoint coefficient `b_grp_time[t]`, trajectory divergence): after t2 the waitlist crosses over, so the **clean randomised contrast lives only at t2** (`b_grp_time[1]`, labelled *causal*); t3/t4 are post-crossover cohort/timing terms. SES excluded (non-DAG / redundant).

:::

## Model

Beta-Binomial$(N, \mu\kappa, (1-\mu)\kappa)$ on the level count, $N = 32$, with
$\operatorname{logit}\mu_{t} = \alpha + \alpha_{\text{time}[t]} + b_{\text{grp}[t]}\text{group} + \gamma_A A_{\text{std},t} + g_{\text{ability}[t]}z(\text{blocks}) + \gamma_{\text{grp\_ability}}\,\text{group}\,z(\text{blocks}) + u_{\text{child}}$.

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Priors

![alpha](prior_alpha.svg){width=24%}
![tau (group)](prior_tau.svg){width=24%}
![gamma_age](prior_gamma_age.svg){width=24%}
![gamma_cross](prior_gamma_cross.svg){width=24%}
![kappa](prior_kappa.svg){width=24%}

## Prior-predictive check

![Prior predictive](prior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## Sampling diagnostics

```{python}
# | echo: false
diag = pd.read_csv(_here / "diagnostics.csv", index_col=0)
diag
```

![Trace](trace_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Energy](energy_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Posterior](posterior_plot.png){.lightbox}

## Posterior-predictive check

![Posterior predictive](posterior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## Findings

### Factor summary

```{python}
# | echo: false
factor_summary
```

`b_grp_time[t]` is the group effect at timepoint $t$ (0=t1 ... 3=t4); only `b_grp_time[1]` (t2) is the clean randomised contrast (**causal**). `gamma_ability_time[t]` is the ability association by timepoint; `gamma_grp_ability` the group$\times$ability cross term. `mean`/`lo`/`hi` = posterior mean + equal-tailed 95% CrI (logit); `prob_positive` = $P(\text{coef}>0)$.

## LOO-PSIS

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print("```\n" + open(_here / "loo.txt").read() + "\n```")
```

## Configuration

```{python}
# | echo: false
config
```
